title: I get to redefine what good mothering means
subtitle: An affirmation for releasing the impossible standard
excerpt: For the moment when the 'good mother' standard feels like something handed down rather than something chosen. A single statement for reclaiming the definition.

practice.whenToUse: When the 'good mother' standard is running the day rather than something you've actually chosen

content.text: The 'good mother' standard is built from other people's ideas: cultural images, your own mother, comparison to other mothers, a vision assembled in childhood. At some point it's worth asking whose standard it is, and whether you want to keep it.
I get to redefine what good mothering means.
Then write one sentence describing what good mothering actually looks like for you. Not the ideal, but your real specific version. Keep it. Read it the next time the standard runs loudly.
